pi_items.source: Web search confirmed ENIAC Wikipedia article exists at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ENIAC
Web search confirmed ENIAC Wikipedia article at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ENIAC matches pattern
Task description specifies this filename; matches verifier.py expectations
Web search confirmed Grace Hopper Wikipedia article exists at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Hopper
Web search confirmed Grace Hopper Wikipedia article at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Hopper matches pattern
Task description specifies this filename; matches verifier.py expectations
Web search confirmed Transistor Wikipedia article exists at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transistor
Web search confirmed Transistor Wikipedia article at https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transistor matches pattern
Task description specifies this filename; matches verifier.py expectations
Task description and setup_task.sh confirm this is the expected manifest location; ga is the task user, Desktop is standard Linux directory

privileged_info_summary: The task targets three real Wikipedia articles: ENIAC (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ENIAC), Grace Hopper (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grace_Hopper), and Transistor (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Transistor). All URL patterns in metadata correctly match the actual Wikipedia article URLs. The task expects agents to generate QR codes from these pages and save them with specific filenames (eniac_qr.png, hopper_qr.png, transistor_qr.png) on the Desktop. A manifest file listing filenames and corresponding URLs must be created at /home/ga/Desktop/qr_manifest.txt. The verifier checks that files exist, are valid images, contain decodable QR codes pointing to the correct Wikipedia articles, and the manifest file contains the expected content.
